title: Vendor in latest containers/storage and vendor/github.com/sirupsen
body:
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>


Need to wait for containers/image.

comments.body:
Please note the switchover to using `golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/terminal` instead of functions that were dropped from the package in the commit log.

Looks like this will need https://github.com/containers/image/pull/319 to be merged first.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 94f2bf0) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@nalind I think you need to take over this patch at this point.  I was trying to do it but got lost in the twisty little passages, around updating version of moby and docker packages.  Is there stuff we can get rid of to make this easier?

@rhatdan I'm not sure that this can be completed currently - the master branch of `github.com/openshift/imagebuilder` with the master branch of `github.com/fsouza/go-dockerclient` pulls in a pinned `github.com/docker/docker/opts` and `github.com/docker/docker/daemon/cluster/convert` which needs `github.com/docker/swarmkit`, which even on its master branch still uses the `logrus` package by its old name.

Cherry-picking https://github.com/nalind/buildah-1/commit/470753cfa1b9751630dd4599434cf179e37acfbe onto your branch will get you as far as I have, if you want to keep at it.
Is there code that we can eliminate or merge into buildah directly?  This seems to be a big mess of packages.
We use `imagebuilder` to drive `build-using-dockerfile`.  After that the details aren't really something we can change here.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably ee91e6b) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
I have dropped the vendored docker content to match containers/image.  Will see if test pass.
@nalind passes tests, but you wanted this to pass more extensive tests correct?
Yeah, I wanted this to go through the tests in #282, as much to give them a workout as anything.
@nalind Tests pass PTAL  Especially at the buildah code.
I'd make a change to `add.go` as above, otherwise LGTM.
The merge commit that Red Hat CI is testing looks a bit off.  Can you rebase, if necessary, and repush?
